basic_information.background: Originally a barefoot doctor in 1930s Guangdong, followed laborers to America. Treated the sick without charge, rumored to have died protecting a fugitive yokai.
narrative_threads: Reunites with a former patient Xu who helps Chinese laborers; becomes a local tutelary deity.
Helps a fisherman before returning to Misty Street; possibly deified for past benevolence.
basic_information.current_status: Runs a quiet herbal clinic on Misty Street; rumored to be a yokai in human form.
basic_information.obsession: Haunted by the idea of people dying alone; aims to ensure no one is uncared for.
basic_information.attitude_toward_human_yokai: Supports peaceful coexistence, intervenes only in times of injustice.
basic_information.appearance: Slender elderly man, faded robes, incense-stained sleeves, fish-scale mark on palm deepens with every saved life.
basic_information.relationships: Respected. Unaware of Chou Hu's identity but knows Hua Pi is a yokai.
